title: salix × polgari
intro: Salix × polgari, also known as Polgar's willow, is a rare hybrid species derived from the willow family (Salicaceae), specifically a cross between Salix alba and Salix fragilis. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various health issues, including inflammation and fever. The bark and leaves of Salix × polgari contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which provides pain relief and reduces inflammation. Herbal preparations of Salix × polgari can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used to alleviate symptoms associated with colds, flu, and other respiratory issues.
